purpose: Visual evidence for duplicate detection. Use these image files before deciding that source images are exact duplicates.
duplicateClassificationRule: Archive only images proven to be exact/same-slice/same-screenshot duplicates or unusable. Caption-only similarity is not enough; uncertain or merely similar images stay primary as recognition reinforcement.

entries.caption: Tc-99m dimercaptosuccinic acid (DMSA) renal cortical scan shows normal uptake in both kidneys. Note the relatively decreased activity in the upper poles .
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Posterior Tc-99m DMSA renal cortical scan shows region-of-interest analysis of bilateral kidneys and background activity , which allows for calculation of relative functioning renal mass.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: On Tc-99m DMSA scans, Mattoo et al suggest separating the kidney into 12 total segments for more consistent reporting between interpreting physicians. This is most consistently done by separating the kidney in half, and then again separating those halves into 2 quadrants. Each quadrant then has 3 segments.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Tc-99m DMSA renal cortical scan shows multiple foci of cortical scar in a patient with vesicoureteral reflux.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Coronal Tc-99m DMSA renal SPECT shows multiple bilateral cortical defects in a patient with bilateral high-grade reflux and multiple previous urinary tract infections (UTIs). Fetal lobulation could also have this appearance.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Coronal Tc-99m DMSA renal cortical scan in a patient with fever, UTI, and vesicoureteral reflux shows multiple photopenic defects that likely represent pyelonephritis and scar .
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Coronal Tc-99m DMSA renal cortical scan shows a rounded lesion in the interpolar left kidney , found to be a cyst on US.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Coronal Tc-99m DMSA renal cortical SPECT shows multiple cortical defects in a patient with acute pyelonephritis.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: 3D Tc-99m DMSA scan shows focally increased activity , suggesting a hypertrophied column of Bertin.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Coronal Tc-99m DMSA renal SPECT shows enlarged central photopenia due to reflux-induced hydronephrosis and a focal defect in the superolateral cortex , consistent with scar.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Anterior Tc-99m glucoheptonate renal cortical scan in a transplant kidney shows multiple cortical defects . It is postulated that acute rejection episodes and renal infarcts cause these defects.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Coronal Tc-99m DMSA renal scan in a left renal transplant shows thinning of cortex in the inferior pole but no frank areas of cortical scarring.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Coronal CECT in the same patient shows minimal renal cortex remaining due to hydronephrosis , explaining the photopenic defect on DMSA.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Posterior Tc-99m DMSA scan in a patient with massive left hydronephrosis demonstrates no left renal cortical uptake , consistent with a nonfunctioning kidney. There is incidental tracer remaining in port at site of injection .
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Coronal fused F-18 FDG PET/CT shows no uptake nor excretion of F-18 FDG in the collecting system of the left kidney , concerning for nonfunctioning kidney. Note the normal right kidney appearance .
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: 3D Tc-99m DMSA renal cortical scan shows normal left renal cortical uptake and a focal photopenic defect in the upper pole of the right kidney , consistent with a scar.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Axial CT shows a large left upper pole renal cyst . Posterior Tc-99m MAG3 scan shows corresponding photopenia in the left upper pole cyst .
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Coronal CECT shows acute renal infarcts in the right kidney of a patient with atrial fibrillation. Posterior Tc-99m MAG3 scan shows cortical defects corresponding to the infarcts.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Coronal fused F-18 FDG PET/CT shows no uptake/excretion in the native kidneys of a patient on hemodialysis. Kidneys demonstrate numerous cysts and calcifications, consistent with polycystic kidney disease.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Axial F-18 FDG PET/CT in a patient with a renal transplant shows diffuse transplant uptake extending into the sinus fat , without tracer excretion. This was diagnosed as posttransplant lymphoproliferative disorder.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Posterior Tc-99m MAG3 scan shows a large cortical defect involving the upper and lower poles of the right kidney.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Coronal MR in the same patient shows a right upper pole enhancing mass and a lower pole simple cyst . Axial MR shows the complex renal mass.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Posterior Tc-99m DMSA renal cortical scan in an adult shows normal uptake in both kidneys. Note the relatively decreased activity in the upper poles , which is within normal limits.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Normal posterior DMSA scan in a 3-year-old patient shows decreased resolution of the columns internally due to small renal size, although there is similar subtle decreased uptake in the superior renal poles .
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: On Tc-99m DMSA scans, Mattoo et al. suggest separating the kidney into 12 total segments for more consistent reporting between interpreting physicians. This is most consistently done by separating the kidney in 1/2 and then again separating those into 2 quadrants. Each quadrant then has 3 segments.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Posterior Tc-99m DMSA renal cortical scan shows region of interest analysis of bilateral kidneys and background activity , which allows for calculation of relative functioning renal mass.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Coronal Tc-99m DMSA renal SPECT shows multiple bilateral cortical defects in a patient with bilateral high-grade reflux and multiple previous urinary tract infections (UTIs). Fetal lobulation could also have this appearance.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Coronal Tc-99m DMSA renal cortical scan in a patient with fever, UTI, and vesicoureteral reflux shows multiple photopenic defects that likely represent a combination of pyelonephritis and scar .
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Coronal Tc-99m DMSA renal SPECT shows a rounded lesion in the interpolar left kidney , found to be a cyst on US.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Coronal Tc-99m DMSA renal SPECT shows multiple cortical defects in a patient with pyelonephritis consistent with acute cortical dysfunction. Follow-up should be considered after antibiotic treatment is complete to evaluate for resolution or persistence of defects.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: 3D Tc-99m DMSA renal scan shows focally increased activity , suggesting a hypertrophied column of Bertin.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Coronal Tc-99m DMSA renal SPECT shows enlarged central photopenia due to reflux-induced hydronephrosis and a focal defect in the superolateral cortex , consistent with scar.

entries.caption: Anterior Tc-99m glucoheptonate renal scan in a transplant kidney shows multiple cortical defects . It is postulated that acute rejection episodes and renal infarcts cause these defects.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Coronal Tc-99m DMSA renal SPECT in a left renal transplant shows thinning of cortex in the inferior pole but no areas of discrete cortical scarring.

entries.caption: Posterior Tc-99m DMSA scan in a patient with massive left hydronephrosis demonstrates no left renal cortical uptake , consistent with a nonfunctioning kidney. There is incidental tracer remaining in the port at the site of injection .

entries.caption: Posterior Tc-99m DMSA in a 3-year-old boy with longstanding left hydronephrosis (see central defect ) secondary to ureteropelvic junction stenosis despite intervention demonstrates minimal residual functioning left renal cortex, which is thin and patchy .
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Coronal fused F-18 FDG PET/CT in a 74-year-old non-dialysis-dependent man with acute-on-chronic acute kidney injury (AKI) demonstrates no renal excretion . High background activity is due to minimal biologic clearance .
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Three color applications of the same posterior Tc-99m DMSA scan show a 10-year-old girl with recurrent pyelonephritis. Some color schemes make defects more apparent (cool jade ), where there are clear cortical defects bilaterally ; some are harder to appreciate (e.g., black and white ).
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Axial NECT (top) shows a large left upper pole renal cyst . Posterior Tc-99m MAG3 scan (bottom) shows corresponding photopenia in the left upper pole cyst .
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Coronal CECT (top) shows acute renal infarcts in the right kidney of a patient with atrial fibrillation. Posterior Tc-99m MAG3 scan (bottom) shows cortical defects corresponding to the infarcts.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: NECT of the abdomen (top) in a patient with fever and AKI shows nonspecific left perinephric stranding . LPO and anterior In-111 WBC (bottom) are negative for renal uptake (would be expected here ), essentially excluding the diagnosis of pyelonephritis.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Axial F-18 FDG PET/CT in a patient with a renal transplant shows diffuse transplant uptake extending into the sinus fat , without tracer excretion. This was diagnosed as posttransplant lymphoproliferative disorder.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Posterior Tc-99m MAG3 scan shows a large cortical defect involving the upper and lower poles of the right kidney.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Coronal MR (top) in the same patient shows a right upper pole enhancing mass and a lower pole simple cyst . Axial MR (bottom) shows the complex renal mass, a presumed renal cell carcinoma.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Coronal SPECT Tc-99m DMSA renal scan shows bilateral striated nephrogram in a patient with bilateral acute pyelonephritis. Areas of decreased uptake correspond to sites of severe inflammation/edema.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Posterior planar glucoheptonate renal scan shows focal absence of activity in the superior pole of the right kidney due to renal abscess, decreased activity in lower pole due to inflammation.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Posterior angiographic (left) and delayed nephrogram phase (right) DTPA renal scan shows abdominal aortic aneurysm that has dissected, infarcting both kidneys, which are photopenic .
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Posterior DMSA renal scan of the kidneys shows infarcted right kidney . Angiogram shows vascular truncation and multiple vascular filling defects (emboli due to atrial fibrillation).
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.
